subject: RE: SOW - Data Dashboarding
rawText:
Hello Romit, Vincent,

 

This is to let you know that my colleague Mriganka Dey has received 2 emailers from automation.cloud@novartis.com last evening.  The 1st contained  credentials to log into the cloud pc and the 2nd with instructions on how to log in . When he tried to log into the account, he was not able to verify with the methods provided.  I  am requesting  support to help with this.

 

Requesting Romit to confirm his availability so we can address this matter and set up the kick off call date / time given that project go live is 1st Dec.
Mriganka would need this initial call to  identify KPIs, understand underlying data and processes, business requirements to create the mock dashboard in phase 1

 

Please do respond to me on the above and your availability to have a call so we can kick start phase 1

 

Regards,

Anuradha

 

From: Anuradha Murthy
Sent: 25 November 2025 11:10
To: 'VINCENT PAUL' <vincent.paul@wipro.com>; Paul, Vincent (Ext) <vincent.paul_ext@novartis.com>;

You don't often get email from automation.cloud@novartis.com. Learn why this is important
	




NextGen Cloud PC: Welcome and Setup Guide


Dear Mriganka Dey,

Welcome to the Novartis Cloud PC family!

Please follow these steps to get started:


You need a Temporary Access Password (TAP) for your first-time login to Cloud PC. Click here select your region, country and contact IT support to obtain your TAP.
Please note that your TAP is valid for eight hours only from the time that it is sent to you and valid for one time use.Ensure to have your Novartis Operations Manager's
name and Project name ready for TAP issuance.For details, contact the requestor who submitted the onboarding request
Connect to the Cloud PC using this link - https://cloudpc.novartis.com
Sign in with your Novartis ID DEYMR2@novartis.net
Once connected, immediately enroll in Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) by following the instructions in this knowledge article. The enrollment link and article are accessible only

You don't often get email from automation.cloud@novartis.com. Learn why this is important
	


Dear Mriganka Dey,

Below is your windows credential to log into your Cloud PC,


Novartis 521 ID = DEYMR2
Initial Network Default Password = idH#IJG68tDbi1_C
After successful login to Novartis Cloud PC, promptly enroll yourself for MFA at go\mfaenroll for successful future logins
Find instructions in Knowledge article

The enrollment link and article are accessible only inside the Cloud PC

Additional Support

If you have any issues logging into your Cloud PC, please contact IT support for your location. Visit http://www.novartis.com/ithelp for contact details
In case of additional queries, please write to the team

Thank you for your cooperation and understanding.

Please Note:This mailbox is not monitored. Please do not reply to this email.?

Best regards,

Data, Digital & IT

subject: Live on: Wednesday, March 4 at 10 AM PT / 1 PM ET
rawText:
You don't often get email from marketing@learn.deepgram.com. Learn why this is important
	


Hi there,

We’re live tomorrow, March 4th at 10 AM PT / 1 PM ET.  

Join our technical webinar on building multilingual voice agents for real-time production systems, led by Deepgram’s Senior Product Manager, Artur Gasparyan, and Staff Research Scientist, Jack Kearney.

 

We’ll cover:

Designing for accents, dialects, and in-session code-switching
Choosing between monolingual and multilingual models
Designing streaming systems that protect latency budgets
Architecting for global scale without operational complexity

If you are building voice systems for customers across languages and regions, this is a session you don’t want to miss. Save your seat and join us live.

 

👉 Reserve your spot now


Live on: Wednesday, March 4 at 10 AM PT / 1 PM ET 
Register Now

subject: Q&A agents answer repeat questions
rawText:
You don't often get email from ivan@mail.notion.so. Learn why this is important
	





Big news today: Custom Agents are finally here! And they’re pretty different from other AI tools you’ve tried.




First, they’re completely autonomous (no manual prompting). Just give them a job, set a trigger or schedule, and they’ll get it done, 24/7. Your agents can automate task triaging, internal Q&A, daily standups and status reports, and even get you to inbox zero.




Second, they're designed for teams. Create and share them together. Control all their permissions, just like a real teammate. And integrate them with the tools your teams already use (Slack, Figma, and more!).





Finally, Custom Agents are super easy to create. Spin one up and let it work while you sleep! Here are a few examples on what they can do:

Try for free

Try Custom Agents on desktop. Mobile is coming soon!

Instead of answering the same questions in Slack manually, over and
